questions: Issue 8 (broken Remotion render): Is the goal to (A) wire server-side rendering via the existing task/worker infrastructure, (B) implement client-side rendering with @remotion/renderer, or (C) just fix the UI to indicate rendering isn't available yet? This fundamentally changes scope — (A) or (B) could be a multi-day effort, while (C) is a 15-minute fix.

Issue 3 (drop zones): Should dropping on an incompatible track silently redirect to the first compatible track (current behavior of getCompatibleTrackId for external drops), or should it reject the drop with visual feedback? Currently internal cross-track drag rejects, but external drag silently finds a compatible track.

Issue 7 (double-click lightbox): Should double-clicking a media clip in the preview overlay open a lightbox showing the source generation, or should it do something else (e.g., open clip properties, start playback from that point)? And should this work for clips that have no generationId (external uploads)?

Issue 4 (pane locking view shifts): Is the fix just adding CSS transitions to smooth the shift, or should the editor pane NOT shift at all (e.g., overlay other panes or use a different layout strategy)?
success_criteria: Dragging a clip to an incompatible track type shows a red/warning visual indicator instead of silently rejecting
Internal (pointer-based) and external (HTML5 drag-drop) drop indicators use consistent visual styling
ClipPanel only shows tabs relevant to the selected clip type (no Text tab for media, no Audio tab for text)
Double-clicking a media clip in the preview overlay opens a lightbox for its source generation
Locking/unlocking adjacent panes while the editor pane is open results in smooth transitions (no content jump)
The Render button behaves correctly per the chosen approach (either triggers a render or clearly indicates unavailability)
All existing drag-drop functionality (gallery → timeline, asset panel → timeline, cross-track) continues to work
No regressions in shared drag-drop utilities (gallery, shots, batch drop zone still functional)
assumptions: The 'broken Remotion render' refers to the startRender stub in useTimelineData.ts:603-606 that only shows a toast — assuming fix approach (C) unless told otherwise, since (A) and (B) require significant new infrastructure
The 'double-click lightbox' issue means we should add lightbox support for media clips in the preview overlay (OverlayEditor), since the only existing double-click handler is for text editing
The 'drop zone' issue covers both visual feedback on incompatible drops AND making drop target areas more discoverable with visual borders during drag
Type mismatch indicators apply to both internal cross-track drag AND external asset/generation drops onto the timeline
The 'text option filtering' means conditionally showing/hiding ClipPanel tabs rather than disabling them
Pane locking view shift fix is a CSS transition smoothing fix, not a fundamental layout change
The 'internal/external drag consistency' issue is about visual indicator styling, not about merging the two drag mechanisms into one (which would require replacing the pointer-based system)
